name: CT Angiography Carotid
description: CT angiography of the carotid arteries for evaluation of carotid stenosis and plaque morphology

findings.technique: CT angiography of the carotid and vertebral arteries was performed from the aortic arch through the circle of Willis.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Aortic Arch
sections.content: The aortic arch demonstrates [normal configuration / no aneurysm / no dissection]. The great vessels originate normally.

sections.name: Right Common Carotid Artery
sections.content: The right common carotid artery demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-69% stenosis / 70-99% stenosis / occlusion]. The vessel wall shows [no plaque / calcified plaque / soft plaque].

sections.name: Left Common Carotid Artery
sections.content: The left common carotid artery demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-69% stenosis / 70-99% stenosis / occlusion]. The vessel wall shows [no plaque / calcified plaque / soft plaque].

sections.name: Right Internal Carotid Artery
sections.content: The right internal carotid artery demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-69% stenosis / 70-99% stenosis / occlusion]. The carotid bulb is [normal / shows plaque].

sections.name: Left Internal Carotid Artery
sections.content: The left internal carotid artery demonstrates [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-69% stenosis / 70-99% stenosis / occlusion]. The carotid bulb is [normal / shows plaque].

sections.name: Vertebral Arteries
sections.content: The bilateral vertebral arteries demonstrate [no stenosis / <25% stenosis / 25-49% stenosis / 50-69% stenosis / 70-99% stenosis / occlusion]. The vertebral arteries are [symmetric / left dominant / right dominant].

sections.name: External Carotid Arteries
sections.content: The bilateral external carotid arteries are [patent / normal]. No significant stenosis.

impression:
1. Normal CT angiography of the carotid arteries.
2. No critical stenosis identified in the bilateral carotid or vertebral arteries.
